content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

April 12, 2016

William M. Wright
The Wright Law Office
2828 Easy Street, Suite 3
Placerville, CA 95667

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-16-055

Dear Mr. Wright:

This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Placer County Local Agency
Formation Commission ("LAFCO") regarding the application of Section 84308 of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Note that the Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission")
does not act as a finder of fact when it renders advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.)
Additionally, our advice is based solely on the provisions of the Act. We offer no opinion on the
applicability of other laws, such as common law conflict of interest.

QUESTION

Is the formation of a Recreation and Park District under Public Resources Code Section
5780 et. seq., on land that is currently owned by 30 landowners with 11 residents which is the
subject of impending development considered a "proceeding involving a license, permit or other
entitlement for use" subject to Section 84308 under the facts provided?

CONCLUSION

Under your unique facts, the formation of a Recreation and Park District is an entitlement
for use and subject to the provisions of Section 84308 because a small group of specific, identifiable
persons will derive financial benefits from and will be directly affected by this decision. Formation
of the district will have a direct substantial financial impact upon the applicants/Placer Vineyards
Development Group LLC due to their small numbers and the application's interlinkage with the
development in the Placer Vineyards Specific Plan area.

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-16-055
Page No. 2

FACTS

The Placer Vineyards project is a proposed development of 13,721 new residential units and
3.6 million square feet of non-residential development on 5,230 acres of land in Southwest Placer
County near Roseville. The planned development will have neighborhood parks, bike paths and
trails.

In February, the Placer County Board of Supervisors adopted a Resolution of Application to
LAFCO to form a new Recreation and Park District to serve the new development described in the
Placer Vineyards Specific Plan Area. There are currently discussions between Placer County and
the property owners in the Placer Vineyards Specific Plan concerning the submission of the
application to LAFCO as contemplated in the development agreement between the County and the
owners.

At this time the property over which the recreation and park district will be formed consists
of approximately five thousand acres, i.e., the entire new development, to fund the district, although
the parkland will be only a portion of that land, around 150 acres. There are approximately 30
landowners and 11 residents who currently own and reside on the undeveloped land. A group of 21
landowners is represented by Placer Vineyards Development Group, LLC, which applied for and
obtained approval for the Placer Vineyards Specific Plan.

The Placer Vineyards Specific Plan Area, once fully developed, is planned for 13,721
residential units serving a population of approximately 31,000, and 3.6 million square feet of non-
residential development. However, the formation of this district will occur prior to full development
of the property.

Additionally, as part of any application, Placer LAFCO requires an "Agreement to Pay
Fees/Costs and Indemnification." Although it is anticipated that Placer County will be the applicant
for the change of organization pursuant to its adopted resolution of application, it is proposed that
Placer Vineyards Development Group, LLC could execute the LAFCO Agreement and reimburse
LAFCO directly, rather than the County, in order to simplify the process.

ANALYSIS

Section 84308 provides in pertinent part:

"(b) No officer² of an agency³ shall accept, solicit, or direct a contribution⁴
of more than two hundred fifty dollars ($250) from any party,⁵ or his or her agent,

___________________________
² "Officer" means any elected or appointed officer of an agency, any alternate to an elected or appointed
officer of an agency, and any candidate for elective office in an agency.

³ "Agency" means an agency as defined in Section 82003 except that it does not include the courts or any
agency in the judicial branch of government, local governmental agencies whose members are directly elected by the
voters, the Legislature, the Board of Equalization, or constitutional officers. However, this section applies to any person
who is a member of an exempted agency but is acting as a voting member of another agency.

File No. A-16-055
Page No. 3

or from any participant,⁶ or his or her agent, while a proceeding involving a
license, permit, or other entitlement for use is pending before the agency and for
three months following the date a final decision is rendered in the proceeding if
the officer knows or has reason to know that the participant has a financial
interest, as that term is used in Article 1 (commencing with Section 87100) of
Chapter 7. This prohibition shall apply regardless of whether the officer accepts,
solicits, or directs the contribution for himself or herself, or on behalf of any other
officer, or on behalf of any candidate for office or on behalf of any committee.

"(c) Prior to rendering any decision in a proceeding involving a license,
permit or other entitlement for use pending before an agency, each officer of the
agency who received a contribution within the preceding 12 months in an amount
of more than two hundred fifty dollars ($250) from a party or from any participant
shall disclose that fact on the record of the proceeding. No officer of an agency
shall make, participate in making, or in any way attempt to use his or her official
position to influence the decision in a proceeding involving a license, permit, or
other entitlement for use pending before the agency if the officer has willfully or
knowingly received a contribution in an amount of more than two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) within the preceding 12 months from a party or his or her agent, or
from any participant, or his or her agent if the officer knows or has reason to
know that the participant has a financial interest in the decision, as that term is
described with respect to public officials in Article 1 (commencing with Section
87100) of Chapter 7.

"If an officer receives a contribution which would otherwise require
disqualification under this section, returns the contribution within 30 days from
the time he or she knows, or should have known, about the contribution and the
proceeding involving a license, permit, or other entitlement for use, he or she shall
be permitted to participate in the proceeding.

"(d) A party to a proceeding before an agency involving a license, permit,
or other entitlement for use shall disclose on the record of the proceeding any
contribution in an amount of more than two hundred fifty dollars ($250) made
within the preceding 12 months by the party, or his or her agent, to any officer of
the agency. No party, or his or her agent, to a proceeding involving a license,
permit, or other entitlement for use pending before any agency and no participant,
or his or her agent, in the proceeding shall make a contribution of more than two
hundred fifty dollars ($250) to any officer of that agency during the proceeding

⁴ "Contribution" includes contributions to candidates and committees in federal, state, or local elections.

⁵ "Party" means any person who files an application for, or is the subject of, a proceeding involving a license,
permit, or other entitlement for use.

⁶ "Participant" means any person who is not a party but who actively supports or opposes a particular decision
in a proceeding involving a license, permit, or other entitlement for use and who has a financial interest in the decision,
as described in Article 1 (commencing with Section 87100) of Chapter 7. A person actively supports or opposes a
particular decision in a proceeding if he or she lobbies in person the officers or employees of the agency, testifies in
person before the agency, or otherwise acts to influence officers of the agency.

File No. A-16-055
Page No. 4

and for three months following the date a final decision is rendered by the agency
in the proceeding. When a closed corporation is a party to, or a participant in, a
proceeding involving a license, permit, or other entitlement for use pending
before agency, the majority shareholder is subject to the disclosure and
prohibition requirements specified in subdivisions (b), (c), and this subdivision."

Your question concerns the definition of "entitlement for use." The term "entitlement for
use" does not have a set legal meaning. The overall scheme and purpose of Section 84308 suggests
that the types of proceedings which should be covered by Section 84308 are those in which specific,
identifiable persons are directly affected or in which there is a direct substantial financial impact
upon the participants. The California courts have examined the term "entitlement for use" in other
contexts. These decisions provide useful guidance; however, interpretation of the Act is not
necessarily limited by interpretation of other laws. (See Section 81013.)

1. Types of proceedings that are subject to the restrictions of Section 84308

• Formation of Special Districts that create an Entitlement for Use. In those situations, where
a special district involves the creation of a special use or benefit to the persons in the
district, the formation proceedings for the district are proceedings which involve an
"entitlement for use" covered by Section 84308. Water, irrigation and similar districts fall
into this category. Thus, whether Section 84308 applies to a special district formation
proceeding depends on the type of district being formed. Special district formation
proceedings are covered only if the special district involves the creation of an entitlement
for use.¹ (Fallon Advice Letter, No. A-85-050.)

• Redevelopment Board Successor Agency payment decisions. Decisions by successor
agencies and oversight boards charged with winding down the affairs of local
redevelopment agencies are subject to Section 84308 when reviewing and deciding payment
of contractual obligations. (De Berry Advice Letter, No. A-12-072.)

• Mobilehome Rent Review Commission decisions on rent increase applications. Section
84308 applies to application to Mobilehome Rent Review Commission for a rent increase
filed by a mobile home park. (Greyson Advice Letter, No. A-09-041.)

• Final discretionary acts of a public agency regarding the development of property. In
Friends of Lake Arrowhead v. Board of Supervisors (1974) 38 Cal.App.3d 497, 509, and
People v. County of Kern (1974) 39 Cal.App.3d 830, 837-840, the final discretionary acts of
a public agency regarding the development of property were considered "entitlements for
use" for purposes of the California Environmental Quality Act ("CEQA"). (Pleines Advice
Letter, No. A-87-220.)

¹ Public Resources Code Section 5780 considers Recreation and Park Districts as a subcategory of Special
Districts. "In enacting this chapter, it is the intent of the Legislature to create and continue a broad statutory authority
for a class of special districts that provides community recreation, park, and open-space facilities and recreation services
within specified boundaries and under local control."

File No. A-16-055
Page No. 5

• Cable TV merger/transfer of control application. For purposes of Section 84308, a Cable Commission's consideration of a merger/transfer of control application is considered a proceeding concerning an entitlement for use. (Steiner Advice Letter, No. A-87-220.)

• Annexations (and Deannexations). Annexations (and deannexations) involve an "entitlement for land use" within the meaning of the law. Annexation is often a necessary step in the processing of large development projects where private financial interests are at stake. It is this type of proceeding where campaign contributions are often perceived as a means of purchasing influence over the decision that the law was intended to cover. (Cf. Woodland Hills Residents Assoc. v. City Council (1981) 26 Cal.3d 938; Fallon, supra; Bradley Advice Letter, No. A-99-258 regarding annexation of property to an irrigation district.)

2. Types of proceedings not subject to Section 84308

Generally, Section 84308 does not cover proceedings where (1) general policy decisions or rules are made or (2) where the interests affected are many and diverse, or (3) there is no actual entitlement to private interests.

A. No entitlements conferred

• Formation of Special Districts that do not create an Entitlement for Use. In those situations, where a special district does not involve the creation of a special use or benefit to the persons in the district, the formation proceedings for the district are not proceedings which involve an "entitlement for use" covered by Section 84308. For example, the formation of school and cemetery districts do not create "entitlements for use" within the meaning of Section 84308. (Fallon, supra.)

• Resource Management Plans ("RMP"). An RMP is intended to serve as a blueprint for the use of certain public lands within the city's jurisdiction, a matter of great importance involving "varied and diverse political and financial interests" throughout the region. You have indicated that the RMP does not grant licenses, permits or entitlements for use to or by any particular person, and there is no "party" applying for adoption of the RMP. To the extent that there are "participants" in this proceeding, they appear to have been drawn from among the general public and represent large and diverse interests. (Larsen Advice Letter, No. A-08-200.)

B. General/Preliminary Policy Decisions or Rules

• Adoption of "Sphere of Influence" Plans. "Sphere of influence" plans are general planning documents adopted by LAFCOs which are intended to guide them in their determination of specific proposals. These types of general plans do not create any "entitlement for use" within the meaning of Section 84308. Thus "sphere of influence" proceedings are not covered by this law. In summary, incorporation and "sphere of influence" proceedings before LAFCOs are not covered by Section 84308. (Fallon, supra.)

File No. A-16-055
Page No. 6

C. Affected Persons are Many and Diverse

• Incorporations. Incorporation proceedings begin with the filing of an application by the
proponents of the new city with the county. Notice is given, and a hearing is held before the
county's LAFCO, which has the power to approve, amend, condition or disapprove the
proposal. As the court noted in Curtis v. Board of Supervisors (1972) 7 Cal.3d 942, the
financial and political interests involved in an incorporation proceeding are varied and
diverse, and the issues directly affect all of the people, businesses and property within the
proposed city boundaries. Therefore, an incorporation is not an "entitlement for use" within
the meaning of Section 84308, and the prohibition and disclosure/disqualifications
requirements of Section 84308 do not apply to LAFCO members in incorporation
proceedings. (Fallon, supra.)

• Formation and Dissolution of a Community Services District ("CSD"). A proceeding
involving the dissolution of CSA is not a proceeding involving an entitlement for use where
the CSA was formed to develop and operate the Apply Valley Airport contained
approximately 1,730 square miles of territory, including all of the territory within the Town
of Apple Valley, the town's sphere of influence, the City of Victorville, the City of
Hesperia, and the City of Adelanto and their respective spheres of influence And also
encompassed unincorporated territory which extended to the Los Angeles County line. The
letter cautioned "[o]f course, this does not mean that all such decisions are exempt from
section 84308. Rather, we must look to each of these types of proceedings on a case-by-case
basis." (Alsop Advice Letter, No. A-04-079.)

In light of the policy purposes and prior advice on Section 84308, we conclude that the
proceeding is subject to Section 84308. Under your facts, there are specific, identifiable persons
who will be directly affected. There are approximately 30 landowners and 11 residents who
currently own and reside on the undeveloped land. A group of 21 landowners is represented by
Placer Vineyards Development Group, LLC, which applied for and obtained approval for the Placer
Vineyards Specific Plan. These numbers are not large or diverse.

Moreover, there will be a direct substantial financial impact upon the applicants, both due to
the small numbers and the interlinkage of the application to the development in the Placer
Vineyards Specific Plan area. In those situations where a special district involves the creation of a
special use or benefit to the persons in the district, the information proceedings for the district are
proceedings which involve an "entitlement for use" covered by Section 84308.⁸ Therefore, the
formation of a Recreation and Park District under your facts is an entitlement for use and subject to
the provisions of Section 84308.

⁸ We recognize that the persons who will ultimate use the parks are diverse and much more numerous than the
applicants currently. However, the analysis of the proceeding must be conducted in light of the current facts.

File No. A-16-055
Page No. 7

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Hyla P. Wagner
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

JWW:jgl

of more than two hundred fifty dollars ($250) from any party, 5 or his or her agent,

officer of an agency, and any candidate for elective office in an agency. 
3 "Agency" means an agency as defined in Section 82003 except that it does not include the courts or any 
agency in the judicial branch of government, local governmental agencies whose members are directly elected by the 
voters, the Legislature, the Board of Equalization, or constitutional officers. However, this section applies to any person 
who is a member of an exempted agency but is acting as a voting member of another agency.  
or from any participant, 6 or his or her agent, while a proceeding involving a

the officer knows or has reason to know that the participant has a financial 
interest, as that term is used in Article 1 ( commencing with Section 87100) of

officer, or on behalf of any candidate for office or on behalf of any committee.

permit or other entitlement for use pending before an agency, each officer of the 
agency who received a contribution within the preceding 12 months in an amount 
of more than two hundred fifty dollars ($250) from a party or from any participant 
shall disclose that fact on the record of the proceeding. No officer of an agency 
shall make, participate in making, or in any way attempt to use his or her official 
position to influence the decision in a proceeding involving a license, permit, or 
other entitlement for use pending before the agency if the officer has willfully or 
knowingly received a contribution in an amount of more than two hundred fifty 
dollars ($250) within the preceding 12 months from a party or his or her agent, or 
from any participant, or his or her agent if the officer knows or has reason to 
know that the participant has a financial interest in the decision, as that term is 
described with respect to public officials in Article l ( commencing with Section 
87100) of Chapter 7.

disqualification under this section, returns the contribution within 30 days from 
the time he or she knows, or should have known, about the contribution and the

be permitted to participate in the proceeding. 
"(d) A party to a proceeding before an agency involving a license, permit, 
or other entitlement for use shall disclose on the record of the proceeding any 
contribution in an amount of more than two hundred fifty dollars ($250) made 
within the preceding 12 months by the party, or his or her agent, to any officer of 
the agency. No party, or his or her agent, to a proceeding involving a license, 
permit, or other entitlement for use pending before any agency and no participant, 
or his or her agent, in the proceeding shall make a contribution of more than two 
hundred fifty dollars ($250) to any officer of that agency during the proceeding 
4 "Contribution" includes contributions to candidates and committees in federal, state, or local elections. 
5 "Party" means any person who files an application for, or is the subject of, a proceeding involving a license, 
permit, or other entitlement for use. 
6 "Participant" means any person who is not a party but who actively supports or opposes a particular decision 
in a proceeding involving a license, permit, or other entitlement for use and who has a financial interest in the decision, 
as described in Article I (commencing with Section 87100) of Chapter 7. A person actively supports or opposes a

and for three months following the date a final decision is rendered by the agency

Your question concerns the definition of"entitlement for use." The term "entitlement for

upon the participants. The California courts have examined the term "entitlement for use" in other

necessarily limited by interpretation of other laws. (See Section 81013.) 
1. Types of proceedings //,at are subject to the restrictions of Section 84308 
• Formation of Special Districts that create an Entitlement for Use. In those situations, where 
a special district involves the creation of a special use or benefit to the persons in the 
district, the formation proceedings for the district are proceedings which involve an 
"entitlement for use" covered by Section 84308. Water, irrigation and similar districts fall 
into this category. Thus, whether Section 84308 applies to a special district formation 
proceeding depends on the type of district being formed. Special district formation 
proceedings are covered only if the special district involves the creation of an entitlement 
for use.7 (Fallon Advice Letter, No. A-85-050.) 
• Redevelopment Board Successor Agency payment decisions. Decisions by successor 
agencies and oversight boards charged with winding down the affairs of local 
redevelopment agencies are subject to Section 84308 when reviewing and deciding payment 
of contractual obligations. (De Ber,y Advice Letter, No. A-12-072.) 
• Mobilelwme Rent Review Commission decisions on rent increase applications. Section 
84308 applies to application to Mobilehome Rent Review Commission for a rent increase 
filed by a mobile home park. (Greyson Advice Letter, No. A-09-041.)

a public agency regarding the development of property were considered "entitlements for 
use" for purposes of the California Environmental Quality Act {"CEQA"). (Pleines Advice 
Letter, No. A-87-220.) 
7 Public Resources Code Section 5780 considers Recreation and Park Districts as a subcategory of Special 
Districts. "In enacting this chapter, it is the intent of the Legislature to create and continue a broad statutory authority 
for a class of special districts that provides community recreation, park, and open-space facilities and recreation services 
within specified boundaries and under local control."  
• Cable TV merger/transfer of control application. For purposes of Section 84308, a Cable 
Commission's consideration of a merger/transfer of control application is considered a 
proceeding concerning an entitlement for use. (Steiner Advice Letter, No. A-87-220.) 
• Annexations (and Deannexations). Annexations (and deannexations) involve an "entitlement 
for land use" within the meaning of the law. Annexation is often a necessary step in the 
processing of large development projects where private ijnancial interests are at stake. It is 
this type of proceeding where campaign contributions are often perceived as a means of 
purchasing influence over the decision that the law was intended to cover. (Cf. Woodland 
Hills Residents Assoc. v. City Council (198 I) 26 Cal.3d 938; Fallon, supra; Bradley Advice 
Letter, No. A-99-258 regarding annexation of property to an irrigation district.) 
2. Types of proceedings not subject to Section 84308 
Generally, Section 84308 does not cover proceedings where ( 1) general policy decisions or 
rules are made or (2) where the interests affected are many and diverse, or (3) there is no actual 
entitlement to private interests. 
A. No entitlements conferred 
• Formation of Special Districts that do not create an Entitlement for Use. In those situations, 
where a special district does not involve the creation of a special use or benefit to the 
persons in the district, the formation proceedings for the district are not proceedings which 
involve an "entitlement for use" covered by Section 84308. For example, the formation of 
school and cemetery districts do not create "entitlements for use" within the meaning of 
Section 84308. (Fallon, supra.) 
• Resource Management Plans ("RMP "). An RMP is intended to serve as a blueprint for the 
use of certain public lands within the city's jurisdiction, a matter of great importance 
involving "varied and diverse political and financial interests" throughout the region. You 
have indicated that the RMP does not grant licenses, permits or entitlements for use to or by 
any particular person, and there is no "party" applying for adoption of the RMP. To the 
extent that there are "participants" in this proceeding, they appear to have been drawn from 
among the general public and represent large and diverse interests. (Larsen Advice Letter, 
No. A-08-200.) 
B. General/Preliminary Policy Decisions or Rules 
• Adoption of "Sphere of Influence" Plans. "Sphere of influence" plans are general planning 
documents adopted by LAFCOs which are intended to guide them in their determination of 
specific proposals. These types of general plans do not create any "entitlement for use" 
within the meaning of Section 84308. Thus "sphere of influence" proceedings are not 
covered by this law. In summary, incorporation and "sphere of influence" proceedings 
before LAFCOs are not covered by Section 84308. (Fallon, supra.) 


C. Affected Persons are Many and Diverse  
• J11co1porations. Incorporation proceedings begin with the filing of an application by the 
proponents of the new city with the county. Notice is given, and a hearing is held before the 
county's LAFCO, which has the power to approve, amend, condition or disapprove the 
proposal. As the court noted in Curtis v. Board of Supe1;1isors (1972) 7 Cal.3d 942, the 
financial and political interests involved in an incorporation proceeding are varied and 
diverse, and the issues directly affect all of the people, businesses and property within the 
proposed city boundaries. Therefore, an incorporation is not an "entitlement for use" within 
the meaning of Section 84308, and the prohibition and disclosure/disqualifications 
requirements of Section 84308 do not apply to LAFCO members in incorporation 
proceedings. (Fallon, supra.) 
• Formation and Dissolution of a Comnumity Services District ("CSD 'J. A proceeding 
involving the dissolution of CSA is not a proceeding involving an entitlement for use where 
the CSA was formed to develop and operate the Apply Valley Airport contained 
approximately 1,730 square miles of territory, including all of the territory within the Town 
of Apple Valley, the town's sphere of influence, the City of Victorville, the City of 
Hesperia, and the City of Adelanto and their respective spheres of influence And also 
encompassed unincorporated territory which extended to the Los Angeles County line. The 
letter cautioned "[ o ]f course, this does not mean that all such decisions are exempt from 
section 84308. Rather, we must look to each of these types of proceedings on a case-by-case 
basis." (Alsop Advice Letter, No. A-04-079.) 
In light of the policy purposes and prior advice on Section 84308, we conclude that the 
proceeding is subject to Section 84308. Under your facts, there are specific, identifiable persons 
who will be directly affected. There are approximately 30 landowners and 11 residents who 
currently own and reside on the undeveloped land. A group of21 landowners is represented by 
Placer Vineyards Development Group, LLC, which applied for and obtained approval for the Placer 
Vineyards Specific Plan. These numbers are not large or diverse. 
Moreover, there will be a direct substantial financial impact upon the applicants, both due to 
the small numbers and the interlinkage of the application to the development in the Placer 
Vineyards Specific Plan area. In those situations where a special district involves the creation of a 
special use or benefit to the persons in the district, the formation proceedings for the district are 
proceedings which involve an "entitlement for use" covered by Section 84308.8 Therefore, the 
formation of a Recreation and Park District under your facts is an entitlement for use and subject to

8 We recognize that the persons who will ultimate use the parks are diverse and much more numerous than the

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Is the formation of a Recreation and Park District under Public Resources Code Section 
5780 et. seq., on land that is currently owned by 30 landowners with 11 residents which is the 
subject of impending development considered a "proceeding involving a license, permit or other 
entitlement for use" subject to Section 84308 under the facts provided?

CONCLUSION: Under your unique facts, the formation of a Recreation and Park District is an entitlement 
for use and subject to the provisions of Section 84308 because a small group of specific, identifiable 
persons will derive financial benefits from and will be directly affected by this decision. Formation 
of the district will have a direct substantial financial impact upon the applicants/Placer Vineyards 
Development Group LLC due to their small numbers and the application's interlinkage with the 
development in the Placer Vineyards Specific Plan area.

embedding.first_500_words: STATE OF CALIFORNIA FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 (916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 April 12, 2016 William M. Wright The Wright Law Office 2828 Easy Street, Suite 3 Placerville, CA 95667 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. A-16-055 Dear Mr. Wright: This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Placer County Local Agency Formation Commission ("LAFCO") regarding the application of Section 84308 of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Note that the Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when it renders advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.) Additionally, our advice is based solely on the provisions of the Act. We offer no opinion on the applicability of other laws, such as common law conflict of interest. QUESTION Is the formation of a Recreation and Park District under Public Resources Code Section 5780 et. seq., on land that is currently owned by 30 landowners with 11 residents which is the subject of impending development considered a "proceeding involving a license, permit or other entitlement for use" subject to Section 84308 under the facts provided? CONCLUSION Under your unique facts, the formation of a Recreation and Park District is an entitlement for use and subject to the provisions of Section 84308 because a small group of specific, identifiable persons will derive financial benefits from and will be directly affected by this decision. Formation of the district will have a direct substantial financial impact upon the applicants/Placer Vineyards Development Group LLC due to their small numbers and the application's interlinkage with the development in the Placer Vineyards Specific Plan area. ¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. File No. A-16-055 Page No. 2 FACTS The Placer Vineyards project is a proposed development of 13,721 new residential units and 3.6 million square feet of non-residential development on 5,230 acres of land in Southwest Placer County near Roseville. The planned development will have neighborhood parks, bike paths and trails. In February, the Placer County Board of Supervisors adopted a Resolution of Application to LAFCO to form a new Recreation and Park District to serve the new development described in the Placer Vineyards Specific Plan Area. There are currently discussions between Placer County and the property owners in the Placer Vineyards Specific Plan concerning the submission of the application to LAFCO as contemplated in the development agreement between the County and the owners. At this time the property over which the recreation and park district will be formed consists of approximately five thousand acres, i.e., the entire new development, to
